title: A first Effect program
subtitle: Describe a computation, then run it — explained line by line

files./src/main.tsx:
import React from 'react'
import { createRoot } from 'react-dom/client'
import App from './App.tsx'
createRoot(document.getElementById('root')).render(<App />)

scenes.narration: Let's write a first **Effect** program — a typed, *lazy* description of a computation — and explain every line.
scenes.say: Let's write a first Effect program: a typed, lazy description of a computation. And I'll explain every line.
We import Effect and pipe from the effect package. Here we load it straight from a C D N so it runs in the browser.

action.type:
import React from 'react'
import { Effect, pipe } from 'https://esm.sh/effect@3'

scenes.narration: `pipe` feeds a value left-to-right through a series of functions.
scenes.say: pipe is a helper that feeds a value left to right through a series of functions. Each step takes the previous result. We open it here.
scenes.cue: feeds a value left to right

scenes.say: Effect.succeed of twenty-one is our starting value. It describes a computation that immediately succeeds with twenty-one. Note: it describes — nothing has actually run.

scenes.narration: `Effect.map` transforms the success value — here, doubling it.
scenes.say: Effect.map takes the success value and transforms it. Here we double it, so twenty-one becomes forty-two. map returns a new Effect; it still has not run.

scenes.narration: Closing the pipe: `program` is just a **description** — nothing has run.
scenes.say: We close the pipe. The result, program, is just a description of what to do. Crucially, nothing has executed yet.

scenes.narration: A component to show the result.
scenes.say: Now a small React component to display the result.

scenes.narration: `Effect.runSync` **executes** the description and returns the value.
scenes.say: Effect.runSync is what finally executes the description, synchronously, and returns the value — forty-two.
A wrapping div styled with Tailwind. min height screen fills the viewport, b g slate fifty is a light background, p eight is padding, and text slate nine hundred is dark text.
A heading. text two x l for size, font bold for weight.
A caption paragraph. m t two adds a little top margin, and text slate six hundred is a muted grey.

action.type: An Effect is a description. runSync executes it.

scenes.say: And the result itself, shown large with text three x l and font bold, so it stands out.

scenes.narration: A classic mistake: using `program` directly — it never runs.
scenes.say: Here's the classic Effect mistake. If you use program directly instead of running it, you get the description object, not the value. Effect is lazy: you must execute it.

diagnostics.message: An Effect is a lazy description, not a value. Execute it with Effect.runSync(program).

scenes.say: The fix is to run it with Effect.runSync. Now result holds the actual number.

scenes.narration: Running live beside the code — `runSync` returns **42**.
scenes.say: And here it runs live, right beside the code. runSync returns forty-two.
